forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure lies over northern SA. A cold front will pass across southeastern SA today before a strong high pressure system moves east mid week and begins to dominate the weather pattern.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers about the agricultural area and Flinders Ranges. Showers more likely about westward facing coasts and ranges and in the southeast. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Northwest to southwesterly winds, fresh about southern coasts and the southeast. Light winds in the north.

Isolated showers about the agricultural area, becoming less likely during the day. Maximum temperatures near to below average. West to southwesterly winds, easing and tending southwest to southeasterly during the day.
Dry. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Dry. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: Generally 2 to 5 mm across the agricultural area, west coast and Flinders Ranges, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the Mount Lofty Ranges.
